implementation_languages: The local snapshot analyzed here is primarily Java 8 with Soot-based analysis. GitHub API metadata checked on 2026-03-13 reports the repository primary language as Python
which likely reflects newer wrapper or auxiliary code rather than this local core engine snapshot.
overall_strengths: Effective on explicit misconfigurations and API misuse patterns
Covers manifest, package assets, WebView, TLS, crypto, storage, and several Android-specific misuse cases in one pipeline
UI-label enrichment is useful: it maps taint flows back to widget ids, `TextView` text, and `EditText` hints, which is valuable for banking-style input-field semantics
Uses Soot on APK directly rather than needing source code
overall_limitations: The local rules are mostly heuristic string/API matches, not deep semantic analysis
Many findings only resolve to class or method granularity
The main pipeline does not actually invoke FlowDroid or IccTA in this snapshot; it only consumes files if they already exist
Several rules are brittle and likely produce false positives or false negatives
Likely false positives or logic issues:
`RSA encryption issue` is emitted even when the app does not use RSA at all
`Screenshot harvest` is a negative global check and can flag apps simply because no `FLAG_SECURE` usage is found anywhere
`Empty Pending Intent` only checks for `PendingIntent`, `0`, and absence of `FLAG_UPDATE_CURRENT`, which is too weak
WebView boolean checks appear to interpret Jimple `>(0)` as enabled, which is likely semantically fragile
`ContentProvider openFile` and `WebView setPluginState` appear to be swapped in implementation
This tool can enrich taint results and classify sink families
However, in this code snapshot, taint generation itself is external and not executed by default in the main path
Without external FlowDroid/IccTA outputs, the taint-related capability mostly disappears
